description_long: This object is a classic brass telescope mounted on a sturdy three-legged wooden tripod. The telescope features a long, cylindrical metallic tube with a slightly flared eyepiece and a narrower objective lens at the opposite end. The tripod legs are slender, dark brown, and splay outward for stability, converging at a central black mounting hub that supports the telescope. The mounting hub appears to allow for rotational adjustment, enabling the telescope to be aimed in different directions. The metallic tube has a smooth, slightly glossy finish, and the tripod legs have a matte wooden texture. This setup is reminiscent of vintage astronomical or surveying equipment, often seen in parks, observatories, or educational urban installations.
description: A vintage brass telescope mounted on a dark wooden tripod with a central black hub, featuring a long metallic tube and splayed legs for stability.
description_view_0: The tripod is viewed head-on from the eyepiece end, showing the alignment of the legs and the circular opening of the telescope.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the full length of the metallic telescope tube and the splayed wooden tripod legs, highlighting the mounting hub.
description_view_2: The tripod is seen from the objective lens end, emphasizing the circular lens and the symmetrical spread of the legs.
description_view_3: Another side view from the opposite direction, showing the telescope tube and tripod legs, mirroring view 1 but from the other side.
